statement.p: Simplify this expression, and if applicable, write the restricted domain.

solution.p: To simplify a complex fraction, we find the common denominator of all fractions. In this case, it is {\left(r+6\right)\mathopen{}\left(r-6\right)}. We multiply the complex fractions numerator and denominator by this common denominator, and we have:
Since r+6 and r-6 were canceled, r \ne 6 and r \ne -6 are lost domain conditions.
